title: oxalis corniculata var. ringoetii
intro: Oxalis corniculata var. ringoetii, commonly known as the wood sorrel, is a variety of the Oxalis corniculata plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of oxalic acid, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The wood sorrel has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as rheumatism, fever, and digestive issues, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Oxalis corniculata var. ringoetii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
